title: JS DOM
description: Learn to select and modify HTML elements using JavaScript DOM methods like querySelector and textContent.

lessons.title: querySelector
lessons.description: Use <kbd>document.querySelector()</kbd> to find the first element matching a CSS selector. It returns a single element you can then modify.
lessons.task: Select the <kbd>h1</kbd> element and store it in a constant called <kbd>title</kbd>

validations.message: Store the result in a constant called <kbd>title</kbd>

lessons.title: textContent
lessons.description: The <kbd>textContent</kbd> property lets you read or change the text inside an element. Setting it replaces all existing text.
lessons.task: Select the <kbd>.msg</kbd> element and set its <kbd>textContent</kbd> to <kbd>"Done!"</kbd>

validations.message: Use the <kbd>textContent</kbd> property to change the text

lessons.title: Inline Styles
lessons.description: Access the <kbd>style</kbd> property to set inline CSS on an element. CSS properties with dashes become camelCase: <kbd>background-color</kbd> becomes <kbd>backgroundColor</kbd>.
lessons.task: Select the <kbd>.box</kbd> element and set its <kbd>style.color</kbd> to <kbd>"coral"</kbd>
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .box { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: bold; }

validations.message: Use the <kbd>.style</kbd> property to set CSS

lessons.title: classList
lessons.description: The <kbd>classList</kbd> property provides methods to add, remove, or toggle CSS classes on an element without touching other classes.
lessons.task: Select the <kbd>.card</kbd> element and add the class <kbd>"active"</kbd> using <kbd>classList.add()</kbd>
lessons.previewBaseCSS: body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1rem; } .card { padding: 1rem; border: 2px solid gray; border-radius: 8px; } .active { border-color: coral; background: #fff0ee; }
